quickAnswer: IIE Varsity College fees for 2026 range from approximately R55,000 to R100,000+ per year depending on the programme and campus. As a private institution, Varsity College does not accept NSFAS, but offers payment plans, the IIE Bursary Scheme, bank student loans (Nedbank, FNB, Absa), and the ISFAP bursary for qualifying students. Contact your nearest Varsity College campus or visit varsitycollege.co.za for your specific programme's fee schedule.
content:
IIE Varsity College Fees 2026

The IIE's Varsity College is South Africa's largest private higher education institution, with campuses in Johannesburg, Pretoria, Cape Town, Durban, Port Elizabeth, and Pietermaritzburg. This page covers IIE Varsity College fees for 2026, including tuition costs by programme, registration fees, payment options, and how to fund your studies since Varsity College does not accept NSFAS.

Varsity College Fees 2026 — Overview by Faculty

Faculty / Programme AreaApproximate Annual Tuition (2026)
BCom Business ManagementR72,000 – R88,000
BCom Marketing ManagementR72,000 – R88,000
BCom Financial ManagementR72,000 – R88,000
BCom Supply Chain ManagementR72,000 – R88,000
LLB (Law)R82,000 – R98,000
BA CommunicationR70,000 – R85,000
BA PsychologyR70,000 – R85,000
BSc IT / Information TechnologyR78,000 – R95,000
BA Education (Foundation Phase)R68,000 – R82,000
Higher Certificate in BusinessR45,000 – R58,000

Note: Varsity College fees vary by campus and are subject to annual adjustment. These figures are estimates for the 2026 academic year. Always confirm exact fees with your chosen Varsity College campus or at varsitycollege.co.za.

Varsity College Additional Fees

Fee TypeApproximate Amount (2026)
Application FeeNo application fee
Registration / Enrolment FeeR3,500 – R6,000 (varies by campus)
Student Levy (SRC, student services)Included in tuition or R1,500 – R2,500
Textbooks & Study MaterialR3,000 – R8,000 per year
Graduation FeeR2,000 – R3,500

Does IIE Varsity College Accept NSFAS?

No — Varsity College does not accept NSFAS funding. NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme) is only available to students enrolled at public higher education institutions. As a private institution, The IIE's Varsity College is not NSFAS-funded.

However, there are several funding alternatives:

How to Fund IIE Varsity College Studies — Alternatives to NSFAS

1. IIE Bursary Scheme

The Independent Institute of Education (IIE) offers its own merit-based bursary scheme for high-achieving students enrolling at Varsity College and other IIE institutions. Apply directly through Varsity College admissions.

2. Varsity College Academic Excellence Scholarship

Students with outstanding matric results (typically 70%+ average) may qualify for academic merit scholarships that reduce tuition fees. Ask your campus admissions office about eligibility criteria.

3. ISFAP (Ikusasa Student Financial Aid Programme)

ISFAP provides bursaries for students from households earning between R350,000 and R600,000 per year — the so-called "missing middle". ISFAP is available at accredited private institutions including IIE. Apply at isfap.ac.za.

4. Bank Student Loans

Several South African banks offer student loans for Varsity College studies:

  • Nedbank Student Loan — Up to R400,000 per year, covers tuition and living expenses
  • FNB Student Loan — Flexible repayment terms after graduation
  • Absa Student Loan — Competitive interest rates for qualifying applicants
  • Standard Bank Student Loan — Available for private institution students

5. Varsity College Payment Plans

Varsity College offers monthly payment plans that allow you to spread your annual tuition fee across the year rather than paying upfront. Contact your campus financial office to set up a payment plan.

6. Employer Bursaries

Many South African companies offer bursaries for students at accredited private institutions. Industries offering employer bursaries include: banking, finance, retail, media, IT, and legal services. Apply directly to companies in your field of study.

Varsity College Campus Fee Offices

CampusLocationPhone
Johannesburg NorthRandburg, Johannesburg0860 00 2777
SandtonSandton, Johannesburg0860 00 2777
PretoriaHatfield, Pretoria0860 00 2777

Durban NorthDurban0860 00 2777
Port ElizabethPort Elizabeth (Gqeberha)0860 00 2777
PietermaritzburgPietermaritzburg, KZN0860 00 2777
Online CampusNationwide (distance learning)0860 00 2777

🔗 View official IIE Varsity College fees → varsitycollege.co.za

Is IIE Varsity College Worth the Cost?

Varsity College's fees are higher than public universities, but the institution offers several advantages:

  • ✅ Nationally accredited IIE qualifications — Recognised by the CHE (Council on Higher Education)
  • ✅ Smaller class sizes — More personalised attention from lecturers
  • ✅ Flexible study options — Including the online campus for working students
  • ✅ Rolling admissions — Apply year-round; no single application deadline
  • ✅ 90%+ graduate employment rate within 6 months of graduating
  • ✅ Strong industry connections — Career support and workplace learning opportunities

Get Extra Academic Support During Your Studies

Whether you're at Varsity College or any other institution, keeping up with your coursework is essential to avoid repeating modules (which adds to your fees). StudyBuddy's AI tutor provides 24/7 academic support for tertiary students, helping you understand difficult concepts, prepare for tests, and improve your grades.

👉 Get 24/7 AI academic support from StudyBuddy →

👉 Find 2026 bursaries and financial aid options →

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform and is not officially affiliated with The IIE's Varsity College or the Independent Institute of Education (IIE). All trademarks belong to their respective owners. Fee information provided is approximate and based on publicly available data — always confirm exact fees at varsitycollege.co.za.

faqs.question: How much are IIE Varsity College fees in 2026?
faqs.answer: IIE Varsity College fees for 2026 range from approximately R45,000 per year for a Higher Certificate to R98,000+ for an LLB or BSc IT degree. Fees vary by programme and campus. Contact your nearest Varsity College campus or visit varsitycollege.co.za for the exact fee schedule for your chosen programme.

faqs.question: Does Varsity College accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: No. IIE Varsity College does not accept NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme) because it is a private institution. NSFAS only funds students at public universities and TVET colleges. Alternative funding options include the IIE Bursary Scheme, ISFAP, bank student loans (Nedbank, FNB, Absa), and Varsity College's own payment plans.

faqs.question: Does IIE Varsity College offer payment plans?
faqs.answer: Yes. IIE Varsity College offers monthly payment plans that allow students to spread their annual tuition fees across the academic year. Contact the financial office at your chosen campus to arrange a payment plan.

faqs.question: What is the ISFAP bursary and can Varsity College students apply?
faqs.answer: ISFAP (Ikusasa Student Financial Aid Programme) provides funding for students from households earning R350,000–R600,000 per year — the 'missing middle' who don't qualify for NSFAS but can't easily afford private institution fees. ISFAP is available at accredited private institutions including IIE Varsity College. Apply at isfap.ac.za.

faqs.question: What other bursaries are available for IIE Varsity College students?
faqs.answer: Varsity College students can apply for: the IIE Bursary Scheme (merit-based), Varsity College Academic Excellence Scholarships (for top matric performers), bank student loans (Nedbank, FNB, Absa, Standard Bank), ISFAP, and employer bursaries from companies in banking, finance, IT, law, and media. Visit our bursaries page for more options.
